title: Multi-thread hierarchical simulation (#2583)
body:
This PoC PR allows multi-thread scheduling inside hierarchical blocks. Number of threads can be specified for each `hier_block` with VLT option:
```
hier_workers -module "<module_name>" -workers "<worker_count>"
```

During the last hierarchical stage, `hier_block`s that have number of workers bigger than 1 are scheduled on smaller thread pool schedules to ensure that there are no shadowing of thread pool worker indexes.

Current limitations:
1. Only a single level nested scheduling is supported. Adding support for nested hierarchical block scheduling could be quite difficult as we would need to evaluate how many threads are actually used by a hier block tree.
2. Size of hierarchical thread pool is the same as `--threads`. This may be extended in the future, as we could easily schedule hierarchical blocks in parallel without hitting slowdown (see https://github.com/verilator/verilator/issues/5776).

Note: provided implementation generates correct simulation code, however it does not fully utilize potential of parallel scheduling when it comes to simulation runtime (about 7.5% gain).

TODO:
- [ ] Optimize scheduling to utilize all threads across all modules

Relevant issue: https://github.com/verilator/verilator/issues/2583.

@gezalore @yTakatsukasa What are your thoughts about this scheduling strategy?
